title: Istio Traffic Management Checklist: Routing, Retries, and Circuit Breaking
description: How to configure traffic management policies in Istio so your services can do canary releases, retry transient failures, and shed load when a downstream service goes bad. Covers VirtualService, DestinationRule, retries, timeouts, circuit breakers, and outlier detection.

items.title: Install Istio with a profile that fits your cluster
items.description: Do not run istioctl install with no flags. The demo profile turns on tracing, telemetry add-ons, and an ingress gateway you may not want in prod. Pick the minimal profile and add components on purpose. This keeps your control plane small and your blast radius predictable.

codeBlocks.label: Install with the minimal profile
codeBlocks.code:
# Inspect what each profile gives you before installing
istioctl profile list
istioctl profile dump minimal

# Install minimal: just istiod, no gateways, no addons
istioctl install --set profile=minimal -y

# Verify the control plane is healthy before doing anything else
istioctl verify-install
kubectl get pods -n istio-system
# NAME                      READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
# istiod-7c8d9f4b9c-xk2p4   1/1     Running   0          45s

items.title: Label namespaces for sidecar injection, never the whole cluster
items.description: Auto-injection at the namespace level keeps you in control of which workloads get a proxy. Inject everywhere by default and you will end up with sidecars in kube-system or in CronJobs that run for 200ms and then sit waiting for the proxy to drain. Opt in per namespace.

codeBlocks.label: Enable injection on a namespace
codeBlocks.code:
# Label the namespace
kubectl label namespace payments istio-injection=enabled

# Restart existing pods so they get the sidecar
kubectl rollout restart deployment -n payments

# Confirm: every pod should now show 2/2 containers
kubectl get pods -n payments
# NAME                       READY   STATUS
# checkout-7d8f9c5b4-jx2pk   2/2     Running
# orders-9f4d8c7b6-mn3qr     2/2     Running

# For a single pod that should NOT get a sidecar (e.g. a Job)
kubectl annotate pod my-job sidecar.istio.io/inject=false

items.title: Define DestinationRule subsets before any routing rules
items.description: VirtualService rules reference subsets by name. If the DestinationRule with those subsets is not in place first, traffic falls back to round-robin across all pods and your canary plan dies on the runway. Always create the DestinationRule first, then the VirtualService.

codeBlocks.label: DestinationRule with version subsets
codeBlocks.code:
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: DestinationRule
metadata:
  name: checkout
  namespace: payments
spec:
  host: checkout.payments.svc.cluster.local
  subsets:
    - name: v1
      labels:
        version: v1
    - name: v2
      labels:
        version: v2
  trafficPolicy:
    loadBalancer:
      simple: LEAST_REQUEST

codeBlocks.label: Verify subsets resolve to real pods
codeBlocks.code:
kubectl apply -f checkout-destinationrule.yaml

# Each subset must match labels on actual pods
kubectl get pods -n payments -l app=checkout --show-labels
# checkout-v1-7d8f9c5b4-jx2pk   ... version=v1
# checkout-v2-9f4d8c7b6-mn3qr   ... version=v2

# Ask Envoy what it sees
istioctl proxy-config cluster $(kubectl get pod -n payments -l app=client -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') \
  -n payments --fqdn checkout.payments.svc.cluster.local

items.title: Set an explicit timeout on every VirtualService route
items.description: Envoy's default request timeout is 15 seconds. That is too long for most APIs and not long enough for some. Without an explicit timeout, a slow downstream will hold connections open and drain your client thread pool. Set a number that matches the SLO of the call, and add it to every route.

codeBlocks.label: VirtualService with timeout
codeBlocks.code:
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: checkout
  namespace: payments
spec:
  hosts:
    - checkout.payments.svc.cluster.local
  http:
    - route:
        - destination:
            host: checkout.payments.svc.cluster.local
            subset: v1
      timeout: 2s   # request budget for this hop

items.title: Configure retries with retryOn, attempts, and perTryTimeout
items.description: A bare retries block retries on 5xx by default with no per-try timeout, which can amplify outages. Pin retryOn to conditions you actually want to retry (gateway-error, connect-failure, refused-stream), set perTryTimeout below your overall timeout, and cap attempts at 2 or 3. Never retry POST writes unless they are idempotent.

codeBlocks.label: Safe retry policy
codeBlocks.code:
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: checkout
  namespace: payments
spec:
  hosts:
    - checkout.payments.svc.cluster.local
  http:
    - route:
        - destination:
            host: checkout.payments.svc.cluster.local
            subset: v1
      timeout: 2s
      retries:
        attempts: 3
        perTryTimeout: 500ms
        retryOn: gateway-error,connect-failure,refused-stream
        retryRemoteLocalities: false   # do not retry to a different zone unless you mean to

codeBlocks.label: Different retry policy for writes
codeBlocks.code:
# Reads can retry. Writes should only retry on connection-level failures.
spec:
  http:
    - match:
        - method:
            exact: GET
      route:
        - destination:
            host: orders
      retries:
        attempts: 3
        perTryTimeout: 1s
        retryOn: 5xx,reset,connect-failure

    - match:
        - method:
            exact: POST
      route:
        - destination:
            host: orders
      retries:
        attempts: 1
        perTryTimeout: 2s
        retryOn: connect-failure   # never retry on 5xx for non-idempotent writes

items.title: Set connection pool limits as your circuit breaker
items.description: In Istio, the circuit breaker is the connectionPool block on the DestinationRule. When concurrent requests or pending connections exceed the limit, Envoy fast-fails new requests with 503 instead of piling up on a struggling pod. Pick numbers from your load tests, not from a tutorial.

codeBlocks.label: DestinationRule with connection pool limits
codeBlocks.code:
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: DestinationRule
metadata:
  name: checkout
  namespace: payments
spec:
  host: checkout.payments.svc.cluster.local
  trafficPolicy:
    connectionPool:
      tcp:
        maxConnections: 100      # per pod
        connectTimeout: 250ms
        tcpKeepalive:
          time: 7200s
          interval: 75s
      http:
        http1MaxPendingRequests: 50    # queue depth before fast-fail
        http2MaxRequests: 200
        maxRequestsPerConnection: 100
        maxRetries: 3                  # cap concurrent retries
        idleTimeout: 30s

codeBlocks.label: Watch the breaker trip
codeBlocks.code:
# Generate load and watch the upstream_rq_pending_overflow counter climb
kubectl exec -n payments deploy/loadgen -c loadgen -- \
  fortio load -c 200 -qps 0 -t 30s http://checkout/api/cart

# Pull stats from the client sidecar
kubectl exec -n payments deploy/loadgen -c istio-proxy -- \
  pilot-agent request GET stats | grep -E 'checkout.*upstream_rq_pending_overflow'
# cluster.outbound|80||checkout.payments.svc.cluster.local.upstream_rq_pending_overflow: 412
# Each overflow is a request Envoy rejected to protect the upstream

items.title: Enable outlier detection to evict bad pods automatically
items.description: Outlier detection is the other half of the circuit breaker. It tracks errors per upstream pod and ejects pods that misbehave for a cooldown period. Without it, a single bad pod (OOM, slow disk, stuck GC) keeps receiving traffic and drags your error rate. Tune consecutive5xxErrors and interval to match your traffic volume.

codeBlocks.label: Outlier detection block
codeBlocks.code:
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: DestinationRule
metadata:
  name: checkout
  namespace: payments
spec:
  host: checkout.payments.svc.cluster.local
  trafficPolicy:
    outlierDetection:
      consecutive5xxErrors: 5      # eject after 5 consecutive 5xx
      interval: 10s                # how often Envoy scans error stats
      baseEjectionTime: 30s        # how long to keep the pod out
      maxEjectionPercent: 50       # never eject more than half
      minHealthPercent: 50         # below this, stop ejecting (avoid death spiral)

codeBlocks.label: Confirm ejections are happening
codeBlocks.code:
# Pull host-level stats from a client sidecar
kubectl exec -n payments deploy/loadgen -c istio-proxy -- \
  pilot-agent request GET clusters | grep -E 'checkout.*(ejected|health_flags)'
# checkout::10.244.1.42:8080::health_flags::/failed_outlier_check
# checkout::10.244.1.42:8080::ejected::true

items.title: Roll out new versions with weighted routing
items.description: Sending 100 percent of traffic to a fresh build is how you discover bugs in production with everyone watching. Start at 5 to 10 percent on the new subset, watch error rate and p99 latency for a few minutes, then ramp. Weighted routing in a VirtualService is the simplest way to do this without an extra controller.

codeBlocks.label: 90/10 canary split
codeBlocks.code:
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: checkout
  namespace: payments
spec:
  hosts:
    - checkout.payments.svc.cluster.local
  http:
    - route:
        - destination:
            host: checkout.payments.svc.cluster.local
            subset: v1
          weight: 90
        - destination:
            host: checkout.payments.svc.cluster.local
            subset: v2
          weight: 10
      timeout: 2s

codeBlocks.label: Ramp the canary in steps

items.title: Use header-based routing to test in prod safely
items.description: Before you give 10 percent of real users to a new version, give it 100 percent of one user: yourself. Match on a header your client sets and route those requests to v2 while everyone else stays on v1. This is the cheapest way to validate a release with production data.

codeBlocks.label: Route on a custom header
codeBlocks.code:
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: checkout
  namespace: payments
spec:
  hosts:
    - checkout.payments.svc.cluster.local
  http:
    - match:
        - headers:
            x-canary-user:
              exact: 'true'
      route:
        - destination:
            host: checkout.payments.svc.cluster.local
            subset: v2
      timeout: 2s

    - route:
        - destination:
            host: checkout.payments.svc.cluster.local
            subset: v1
      timeout: 2s

codeBlocks.label: Test the route

items.title: Mirror traffic to validate v2 without serving its responses
items.description: Mirroring sends a copy of every live request to a second destination and throws away the response. The user only ever sees the primary's answer, so you can hammer v2 with real shapes of real traffic and check its logs for errors before sending any user to it. Pair with a percentage so you do not double your downstream load.

codeBlocks.label: Mirror 25 percent of traffic to v2
codeBlocks.code:
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: checkout
  namespace: payments
spec:
  hosts:
    - checkout.payments.svc.cluster.local
  http:
    - route:
        - destination:
            host: checkout.payments.svc.cluster.local
            subset: v1
          weight: 100
      mirror:
        host: checkout.payments.svc.cluster.local
        subset: v2
      mirrorPercentage:
        value: 25.0

items.title: Inject faults to test that retries and breakers actually work
items.description: You wrote a retry policy and a circuit breaker. Now prove they work before the next outage proves they do not. Istio can inject HTTP errors and delays at the proxy with no code change. Run these in a non-prod namespace and watch your client's behavior.

codeBlocks.label: Inject 50 percent 503 errors
codeBlocks.code:
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: checkout-fault-test
  namespace: payments-staging
spec:
  hosts:
    - checkout
  http:
    - fault:
        abort:
          percentage:
            value: 50.0
          httpStatus: 503
      route:
        - destination:
            host: checkout
            subset: v1

codeBlocks.label: Inject a 5 second delay
codeBlocks.code:
spec:
  http:
    - fault:
        delay:
          percentage:
            value: 100.0
          fixedDelay: 5s
      route:
        - destination:
            host: checkout
            subset: v1
# Run your client. With a 2s timeout and 3 retries you should see
# fast-failure, not 5s hangs.

items.title: Run istioctl analyze before applying any config
items.description: VirtualService and DestinationRule errors do not always show up at apply time. A typo in a subset name, a host that resolves to nothing, a missing namespace prefix: kubectl will accept all of these. istioctl analyze catches them and tells you what is broken in plain English. Wire it into CI so bad config never reaches the cluster.

codeBlocks.label: Analyze a single namespace
codeBlocks.code:
# Catch issues across the live config plus a file you are about to apply
istioctl analyze -n payments checkout-virtualservice.yaml

# Example output:
# Error [IST0101] (VirtualService payments/checkout)
#   Referenced host+subset in destinationrule not found:
#   "checkout+v3"
# Error: Analyzers found issues when analyzing namespace: payments

# Run across all namespaces in CI
istioctl analyze --all-namespaces --suppress 'IST0118=*'

codeBlocks.label: Pre-merge CI check
codeBlocks.code:
# .github/workflows/istio-validate.yaml
name: istio-validate
on: [pull_request]
jobs:
  analyze:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Install istioctl
        run: |
          curl -L https://istio.io/downloadIstio | sh -
          sudo mv istio-*/bin/istioctl /usr/local/bin/
      - name: Analyze changed manifests
        run: |
          istioctl analyze --recursive ./manifests/istio/ \
            --failure-threshold Warning

items.title: Watch the right metrics so you know when policies fire
items.description: If you cannot see your retries, timeouts, and ejections in a dashboard, you cannot tune them. Pull Envoy stats into Prometheus and put four signals in front of yourself: request rate per subset, p99 latency, 5xx ratio, and pending-request overflow. Add Kiali for the topology view when you need to debug routes by hand.

codeBlocks.label: PromQL for the four signals
codeBlocks.code:
# Request rate per destination subset
sum by (destination_workload, destination_version) (
  rate(istio_requests_total{reporter="source"}[1m])
)

# 5xx ratio per destination
sum by (destination_workload) (
  rate(istio_requests_total{response_code=~"5.."}[5m])
) /
sum by (destination_workload) (
  rate(istio_requests_total[5m])
)

# p99 latency per destination
histogram_quantile(0.99,
  sum by (destination_workload, le) (
    rate(istio_request_duration_milliseconds_bucket[5m])
  )
)

# Circuit breaker fast-fails (pending overflow)
sum by (cluster_name) (
  rate(envoy_cluster_upstream_rq_pending_overflow[5m])
)

codeBlocks.label: Quick visual debugging with Kiali
codeBlocks.code:
# Port-forward Kiali and look at the traffic graph
kubectl port-forward -n istio-system svc/kiali 20001:20001

# Open http://localhost:20001
# Versioned graph + edge labels show:
#   - actual percentage going to each subset
#   - which routes are returning 5xx
#   - whether your VirtualService matches what you intended
